title: Pipeline bottleneck elimination - numerically-neutral, validated against M1 reference
registered_by: user directive: adapt pipelines so there is no more bottleneck
principle: Time-axis path simulation stays mathematically serial (prev_weights + compounding equity dependency). We do NOT parallelize across time (would change numbers). Instead we shrink per-step cost and parallelize across variants. Every change MUST reproduce strategy_daily_returns.csv bit-identically vs the M1 reference before use.
hard_invariant: strategy_daily_returns.csv MUST be byte/float-identical to full-mode M1 reference. Fail-closed: if it differs, the optimization is rejected.

diagnosis.phase_a_ml: already parallel (32 workers); not the bottleneck
diagnosis.phase_b_serial: the bottleneck. Per-rebalance work = (A) constraint application -> executed weights (REQUIRED, affects returns, serially dependent on prev_weights) + (B) decision_rows + diagnostics (NOT affecting returns).

optimizations.change: Skip decision_rows / weight_rows / portfolio_diagnostics overhead in Phase B; returns path unchanged.
optimizations.validate: bit-identical strategy_daily_returns.csv vs M1 reference before AA_POST_M1_PERF=1 in production

optimizations.change: Save path_sim_checkpoint.pkl every 25 rebalances; resume after hang/kill.
optimizations.validate: resume produces identical CSV vs uninterrupted run

optimizations.change: Load prices/features/returns once; fan out N variants across the idle cores instead of N independent processes each re-loading data.
optimizations.validate: each variant's returns identical to its standalone run

optimizations.change: Profile-driven numerically-neutral hot-loop cleanups (cache invariants, avoid per-rebalance rebuilds).

gating.reference_returns_needed_first: M1 sealed -> provides the per-variant reference CSVs to validate against
execution_order: use for M3 / daily-alpha heavy runs
